titleStmt.title: The Minister in Bulgaria ( (Earle) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Minister in Bulgaria ( Earle ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 53. The Greek MinisterPanayotis Pipinélis. is worried about the attacks on Greece by the German press. Since it is the first time these have occurred in anything like their present intensity he fears it is a prelude to action.

The British MinisterGeorge William Rendel. informs me that much work at high speed is being done in Bulgaria to widen and strengthen the road to and through the Struma gorge leading to Greece. The British Military Attaché says that he is packing preparatory to leaving this country, the chief of the British Intelligence Service here says facetiously he is 50% certain that the Germans will pass through Bulgaria and 50% more than certain.
The Foreign MinisterIvan Vladimir Popoff, Bulgarian Minister for Foreign Affairs. alone maintains an attitude of tempered optimism.

I have seen personally some of the thoroughness of the German preparations for passage through Bulgaria. It is still possible that they are to be used only as a threat against Greece and Turkey. But it is also certain that should the threat fail German troops could
reach the Bulgarian-Greek border through Bulgaria, according to the British Military Attaché, in 10 days or less.
